title: Retrieve All Items: - Rotate Focus: Review All Parts of a List Instead of
subtitle: Overcome Memory Inhibition
description:
To retrieve all items:
- Rotate focus: Review all parts of a list instead of sticking to the first items.
- Use triggers: Write down clues to jog your memory for the forgotten parts.
- Repeat evenly: Go over the list multiple times without overemphasizing certain items.

Example: When revising notes, review sections evenly instead of just re-reading the first few pages.

howTo.title: Prepare your list
howTo.summary: Gather the items you want to review, ideally between 8 to 12. Open the Brali LifeOS Rotate Focus task or write the header on paper. Create a visual representation of the list to keep it in view.

howTo.title: Partition the items
howTo.summary: Break the list into manageable chunks of 2 to 6 items. This helps reduce cognitive load and makes it easier to rotate focus during review.

howTo.title: Create retrieval cues
howTo.summary: For each item, write a short verbal clue and add a visual tag. This multi-modal approach enhances memory retrieval by creating multiple pathways.

howTo.title: Rotate and recall
howTo.summary: Set a timer and conduct multiple passes over the list in a non-linear order. Use the cues to recall each item, taking short breaks between passes.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results in the Brali LifeOS app. Record the number of items recalled and the total time spent on the session at https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/rotate-focus-list-recall/.
